channel.title: PINtPOINT Blog
channel.description: Long-form writing from the PINtPOINT team on beer, pubs, and the tools we build. Love letters to beer cities, guides to crawls and styles, and notes on what's new in the app.

item.title: The Whippet: Why This Could Be One of London's Most Interesting Beer Openings of 2026
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Bloomsbury Leisure Group — the team behind the Euston Tap — opens The Whippet outside Liverpool Street on Thursday 14 May 2026. Augustiner on tap, Boddingtons cask with a sparkler, opening brewery line-up of independent regionals (Green Duck, Bris Beer Factory, Joseph Holt, Floc, Otter, Ossett). The first new site under the Whippet name since the Holborn original closed in 2020.

item.title: Radio City Turns 7: 7 Cans, 7 Sins, 35 Launch Venues — and One Big Saturday in Chelmsford
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Saturday 9 May 2026 — Radio City Beer Works marks its seventh birthday with a brewery party in Radley Green, preceded by the nationwide can release of The 7 Deadly Sins on Thursday 7 May across 35 first-pour venues UK-wide. Brewery party logistics, full sin × brewery × style line-up, and the launch-venue locator.

item.title: Blackhorse Beer Mile 4th Birthday: All 10 Participating Venues with PINtPOINT Links
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Sunday 3 May 2026 — Walthamstow's brewery-district mile turns four with an all-day, free-entry, family-friendly takeover across 10 east-London craft venues. Every participating venue with a PINtPOINT deeplink plus the canonical Hall of Fame crawl.

item.title: Augustiner on Draft, Finally: Six Confirmed UK Pubs Pouring It
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Augustiner Helles launched on UK draught in November 2025 via James Clay & Sons with no public venue list. PINtPOINT cross-references public Untappd data to confirm six UK pubs pouring it — across London, Manchester, Newcastle, Luton and Glasgow — with a seventh, the Whippet, opening 14 May.

item.title: 12 Years of Bosko: All 12 UK Venues Pouring Pressure Drop's West Coast IPA on 1 May 2026
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Twelve years of Pressure Drop's iconic West Coast IPA, celebrated 1 May 2026 with simultaneous pours at 12 UK venues. Full launch-venue list with PINtPOINT links and back-story on Bosko.

item.title: Left Field Beer: One of Britain's Best Independent Beer Shops
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Fresh American imports (Trillium, Hop Butcher), European specialists (Arpus, CoolHead, Factory) and championed UK indies (DEYA, Buxton), curated from a single Solihull shop — with the deep-dive on why drinking American craft properly is so expensive over here.

item.title: A Love Letter to London: The City That Taught the World to Brew, Then Started Again
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: London is one of the oldest beer cities in the world, but the version you can drink now is barely fifteen years old. The third in the love-letter trilogy — a comeback layered over an inheritance.

item.title: Walking the Ted Lasso Pub Trail in Richmond — From Crown & Anchor to Richmond Hill
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: The Crown & Anchor exterior is The Prince's Head on Richmond Green. The view above the Thames bend is the one Ted reflects from. A four-stop walking pub trail for fans of the show — and a shortlist of the wider Richmond craft-beer scene worth a pint while you're there.

item.title: A Love Letter to Melbourne: The City That Built a Craft-Beer Scene on Bars, Not Breweries
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Companion to the San Diego piece — the structural opposite. Catfish, Local Taphouse, Cookie, Hippo, Boilermaker — a scene that turned curation into a category of its own.

item.title: The Drinker's Guide to Hops: What Each Variety Actually Tastes Like
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: A drinker-first reference to the 30-odd hops you're most likely to meet on a UK or European bar — Cascade, Citra, Mosaic, EKG, Saaz and the rest, organised by a six-category flavour framework.

item.title: A Love Letter to San Diego: How a Navy Town Became the IPA Capital of the World
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Karl Strauss in 1989. Stone and Ballast Point in 1996. A hop arms race through the 2000s that perfected the West Coast IPA. A tour of how San Diego quietly became the IPA capital and where to drink its legacy today.

item.title: The Chelmsford Beer Mile: A Live Guide to Essex's Best Craft-Beer Crawl
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: Eight specialist venues in a mile — bottle shops, a brewpub, railway arch taprooms — walked in the right order. With live tap lists for every stop, plus a footnote on Baddow Brewery (1798–1927) and how Chelmsford Brew Co broke the century-long quiet.

item.title: What's Wrong With Beer Recommendation Systems (And What PINtPOINT Does Differently)
item.dc:creator: Sophie Ro
item.description: A response to Ethan Haley's Untappd-as-a-recommender analysis and NYC Data Science's Ninkasi project. Three walls rating-based recommenders hit — cold start, popularity bias, geography-blindness — and how PINtDEXTER layers around them.
